---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---

```{r}
 library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
Blood_datas <- read_excel("antibody_datas.xlsx")
```



```{r}
Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    `Shark_Id` = `Sharks Id`
  )
```

```{r}
Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    esr        = `ESR (mm/h)`
  )

```


```{r}
Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Shark_Id))

```





```{r}
cat("Original rows:", nrow(Blood_datas), "\n")
cat("Cleaned rows:", nrow(Blood_datas), "\n")
head(Blood_data_clean)
```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Antibody_titer is numeric
Blood_datas<- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(Antibody_titer = as.numeric(Antibody_titer))

# Check for NAs after conversion (in case there were non-numeric values)
sum(is.na(Blood_datas$Antibody_titer))

# Calculate mean and SD per week
summary_data <- Blood_datas %>%
  group_by(week) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_titer = mean(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_titer = sd(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Create barplot with error bars
barplot_Antibody_titer <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = factor(week), y = mean_titer)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "lightblue", color = "darkblue") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_titer - sd_titer, ymax = mean_titer + sd_titer), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Antibody_titer by Week ± SD",
    x = "Week Number",
    y = "Mean Antibody Titer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(barplot_Antibody_titer)
```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Antibody_titer is numeric
Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(Antibody_titer = as.numeric(Antibody_titer))

# Check for NAs after conversion
sum(is.na(Blood_datas$Antibody_titer))

# Calculate mean and SD per group
summary_data <- Blood_datas %>%
  group_by(Groupe) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_titer = mean(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_titer = sd(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Create barplot with error bars
barplot_Antibody_titer <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = factor(Groupe), y = mean_titer)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "lightgreen", color = "darkgreen") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_titer - sd_titer, ymax = mean_titer + sd_titer), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Antibody_titer by Group ± SD",
    x = "Group",
    y = "Mean Antibody Titer"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(barplot_Antibody_titer)


```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

# Ensure Antibody_titer is numeric
Blood_datas<- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(Antibody_titer = as.numeric(Antibody_titer))

# Check for NAs after conversion
sum(is.na(Blood_datas$Antibody_titer))

# Calculate mean and SD per group per week
summary_data <- Blood_datas %>%
  group_by(week, Groupe) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_titer = mean(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_titer = sd(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop'
  )

# Create grouped barplot with error bars
grouped_barplot <- ggplot(summary_data, aes(x = factor(week), y = mean_titer, fill = Groupe)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), color = "black") +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean_titer - sd_titer, ymax = mean_titer + sd_titer),
                position = position_dodge(width = 0.8), width = 0.2, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Antibody_titer by Week and Group ± SD",
    x = "Week",
    y = "Mean Antibody Titer",
    fill = "Group"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(grouped_barplot)

```

```{r}
Blood_data_clean %>%
  summarise(shapiro_p = shapiro.test(Antibody_titer)$p.value)
```
```{r}
library(car)

# Levene's Test for homogeneity of variances across groups
leveneTest(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_data_clean)
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
 library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)

qq_plots_titer <- Blood_datas %>%
  # Convert week to a factor for proper grouping
  mutate(week_factor = factor(week)) %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(sample = Antibody_titer)) +
  # Create a separate panel for every unique Groupe/week combination
  facet_grid(Groupe ~ week_factor) +
  # Add the plot points and the normality line
  stat_qq() + 
  stat_qq_line() +
  labs(
    title = "QQ Plots for Antibody_titer: Normality Check by Groupe and Week",
    x = "Theoretical Quantiles (Normal Distribution)",
    y = "Sample Quantiles (Antibody Titer, log2)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 12)
  )

print(qq_plots_titer)

```


```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(tidyverse)
library(writexl)
library(DHARMa)

Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

Blood_datas$Groupe[Blood_datas$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Control"

lmm_Ab <- lmer(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
               data = Blood_datas)

summary(lmm_Ab)

# ----------------------------
# 2. Residual diagnostics
# ----------------------------
res_Ab <- simulateResiduals(lmm_Ab, n = 1000)
plot(res_Ab)
testUniformity(res_Ab)
testDispersion(res_Ab)
testZeroInflation(res_Ab)

# ----------------------------
# 3. Post-hoc comparisons
# ----------------------------
emms_Ab <- emmeans(lmm_Ab, ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor)

# Function to create Excel-friendly pairwise table
prepare_emmeans_df <- function(emms_obj) {
  df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emms_obj, adjust = "tukey"))
  
  df <- df %>%
    separate(contrast, into = c("Group1", "Week1", "minus", "Group2", "Week2"), sep = " ") %>%
    select(-minus) %>%
    mutate(
      Direction = ifelse(estimate > 0, "Group1 > Group2", "Group2 > Group1"),
      Significant = ifelse(p.value < 0.05, "Yes", "No")
    )
  
  return(df)
}

Ab_pairs_df <- prepare_emmeans_df(emms_Ab)

# ----------------------------
# 4. Export to Excel
# ----------------------------
write_xlsx(Ab_pairs_df, "Antibody_Pairwise.xlsx")
```
```{r}
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(emmeans)
library(tidyverse)
library(writexl)
library(DHARMa)

# --- 0. Data Preparation and Rank Transformation ---

# 1. Apply Rank Transformation to the zero-inflated variable
# CRITICAL FIX: Create the rank column
Blood_datas$Antibody_titer_rank <- rank(Blood_datas$Antibody_titer)

# 2. Ensure factors are set
Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

# 3. Clean Group Name
Blood_datas$Groupe[Blood_datas$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Control"


# --- 1. Fit the LMM to Rank-Transformed Data ---

# CRITICAL FIX: Use the rank-transformed variable
lmm_Ab_rank <- lmer(
  Antibody_titer_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_datas
)

print("--- Summary of Rank-LMM for Antibody Titer ---")
summary(lmm_Ab_rank)


# ----------------------------
# 2. Residual diagnostics
# ----------------------------

print("\n--- DHARMa Diagnostics for Rank-LMM ---")
res_Ab_rank <- simulateResiduals(lmm_Ab_rank, n = 1000)

# Plotting residuals (will open a separate plot window)
plot(res_Ab_rank)

testUniformity(res_Ab_rank)
testDispersion(res_Ab_rank)
testZeroInflation(res_Ab_rank)


# ----------------------------
# 3. Post-hoc comparisons (Tukey-adjusted)
# ----------------------------

# Use the rank model output
emms_Ab_rank <- emmeans(lmm_Ab_rank, ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor)

# Function to create Excel-friendly pairwise table (Modified for simplicity and clarity)
prepare_emmeans_df <- function(emms_obj) {
  df <- as.data.frame(pairs(emms_obj, adjust = "tukey"))
  
  # Select and rename columns for clarity
  df <- df %>%
    select(contrast, estimate, SE, df, t.ratio, p.value) %>%
    rename(`P-Value` = p.value)
  
  return(df)
}

Ab_pairs_df <- prepare_emmeans_df(emms_Ab_rank)
print("\n--- Post-hoc Pairwise Comparisons (Ranked Data) ---")
print(Ab_pairs_df)

# ----------------------------
# 4. Export to Excel
# ----------------------------
# CRITICAL FIX: Update filename to reflect Rank-LMM
write_xlsx(Ab_pairs_df, "Antibody_Titer_Rank_Pairwise.xlsx")

print("\nSuccessfully exported: Antibody_Titer_Rank_Pairwise.xlsx")
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(emmeans)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Compute estimated marginal means (averaging over sex)
Blood_datas$Groupe[Blood_datas$Groupe == "Contrôle"] <- "Control"

Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe))


emmeans_output <- emmeans(glmm_model, 
                          ~ week_factor * Groupe_factor)

# 2. Convert to data frame
emmeans_df <- as.data.frame(summary(emmeans_output))
emmeans_df$Groupe_factor <- emmeans_df$Groupe_factor %>%
  fct_recode(Control = "Contrôle")

line_plot <- ggplot(emmeans_df, aes(x = week_factor, y = emmean,
                                    color = Groupe_factor, group = Groupe_factor)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = emmean - SE, ymax = emmean + SE), width = 0.1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(
    "Control" = "#D6C9AE",
    "Winter"  = "#6F9FCC",
    "Summer"  = "#D97A66"
  )) +
  theme_bw()

print(line_plot)
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr) # Already loaded if using the previous code blocks

# --- 1. Data Preparation and Factor Setup ---

Blood_datas <- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(
    # Create the descriptive factor for weeks for better labels
    week_label = factor(
      paste("Week", week), 
      levels = c("Week 4", "Week 6", "Week 8") # Ensure the order is correct
    ),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe)
  )

# --- 2. Create the Final Box Plot ---

p_Ab_final <- ggplot(
  Blood_datas, 
  aes(x = Groupe_factor, y = Antibody_titer, fill = Groupe_factor) # X-axis is now Group
) +
  # Use geom_boxplot
  geom_boxplot(
    outlier.shape = 19, # Use solid black dots for consistency
    outlier.colour = "black", 
    outlier.size = 2
  ) +
  
  # --- Facet by Descriptive Week Label, moved to the BOTTOM ---
  facet_wrap(
    ~ week_label, 
    scales = "fixed", 
    strip.position = "bottom" # Moves the labels below the panels
  ) +
  
  # Apply Custom Colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Control" = "#D6C9AE",
    "Winter" = "#6F9FCC",
    "Summer" = "#D97A66"
  )) +
  
  # Labels and Theme Adjustments
  labs(
    title = "", # CRITICAL FIX: Removed Plot Title
    x = "", # CRITICAL FIX: Removed X-axis label ("Week")
    y = "Antibody Titer",
    fill = "" # CRITICAL FIX: Removed Legend Title
  ) +
  
  # Use a clean theme
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
        # Remove the x-axis labels (Control, Summer, Winter) 
        # as they are confusing since they repeat under each facet
        axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks.x = element_blank(),
        
        # Adjust facet strip to be at the bottom and clean up background
        strip.placement = "outside",
        strip.background = element_blank(), 
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12), 
        
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.spacing = unit(0.5, "lines"),
        
        # Center the overall plot (optional, but good practice)
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5) 
  )

print(p_Ab_final)
```




```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(rstatix)

# Weeks to test
weeks_to_test <- sort(unique(Blood_datas$week))

# Prepare results storage
kw_results <- tibble(
  Week = integer(),
  Statistic = double(),
  df = double(),
  p_value = double()
)

# Loop through weeks
for(wk in weeks_to_test){
  week_data <- Blood_datas %>% filter(week == wk)
  
  kw <- kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = week_data)
  
  kw_results <- kw_results %>% 
    add_row(
      Week = wk,
      Statistic = kw$statistic,
      df = kw$parameter,
      p_value = kw$p.value
    )
}

# Print results
kw_results

# Optional: save to Excel
write_xlsx(kw_results, "Kruskal_Antibody_Weekwise.xlsx")

```
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(rstatix)

# Kruskal-Wallis test across all weeks
kw_overall <- kruskal_test(Blood_datas, Antibody_titer ~ Groupe)
kw_overall

# Optional: post-hoc pairwise comparisons using Dunn test
pairwise_dunn <- Blood_datas %>%
  dunn_test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, p.adjust.method = "bonferroni")
pairwise_dunn

# Optional: save to Excel
library(writexl)
write_xlsx(list(
  KW_Overall = kw_overall,
  Pairwise_Dunn = pairwise_dunn
), "Antibody_Overall_Groups.xlsx")

```


```{r}
kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_datas %>% filter(week == 4))
kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_datas %>% filter(week == 6))
kruskal.test(Antibody_titer ~ Groupe, data = Blood_datas %>% filter(week == 8))
```

```{r}
Blood_data_female <- Blood_data_clean %>%
  filter(Sex_factor == "Female")
```

```{r}
# Convert categorical variables to factors if not already
 Blood_data_female <- Blood_data_female %>%
  mutate(
    week_factor = factor(week),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)  # replace with your shark ID column name
  )

# Fit the GLMM using lmer (treating log2 titers as continuous)
glmm_model_1 <- lmer(
  Antibody_titer ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + eggs_presence+ (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_data_female
)

# View the model summary (lmerTest gives p-values)
summary(glmm_model_1)

# Calculate marginal and conditional R²
r.squaredGLMM(glmm_model_1)

# Simulate residuals using DHARMa
simulation_output_1 <- simulateResiduals(fittedModel = glmm_model_1, n = 250)

# Plot diagnostic checks (3 panels: uniformity, QQ, dispersion)
plot(simulation_output_1)

```
```{r}
library(openxlsx)

# Create a data.frame with your fixed effects
fixed_effects_table <- data.frame(
  Effect = c("(Intercept)", "Groupe_factorSummer", "Groupe_factorWinter",
             "week_factor6", "week_factor8", 
             "Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor6", "Groupe_factorWinter:week_factor6",
             "Groupe_factorSummer:week_factor8", "Groupe_factorWinter:week_factor8"),
  Estimate = c(3.4119, -0.4873, 1.8231, 0.9981, 1.9972, -1.3331, -1.4435, -1.8913, -3.1025),
  Std_Error = c(1.0598, 1.7089, 1.4988, 0.7012, 0.7012, 1.1307, 0.9917, 1.1916, 0.9917),
  df = c(24.2561, 24.2561, 24.2561, 35.0401, 35.0401, 35.0401, 35.0401, 35.3267, 35.0401),
  t_value = c(3.219, -0.285, 1.216, 1.423, 2.848, -1.179, -1.456, -1.587, -3.129),
  P_value = c(0.00363, 0.77796, 0.23555, 0.16346, 0.00731, 0.24633, 0.15440, 0.12138, 0.00353)
)

# Export to Excel
write.xlsx(fixed_effects_table, "Fixed_Effects_Table.xlsx", rowNames = FALSE)
```


```{r}
library(dplyr)

group_summary <- Blood_datas_clean %>%
  group_by(Groupe_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    n    = n(),
    .groups = "drop"   # removes grouping after summarise
  )

group_summary

# --- Mean ± SD by Group and Week ---
group_week_summary <- Blood_datas_clean %>%
  group_by(Groupe_factor, week_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    n    = n(),
    .groups = "drop"   # ensures the result is ungrouped
  )

group_week_summary
```


```{r}

library(lme4)
library(emmeans)
library(multcomp)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)

# ----------------------------
# 0) Prepare data & factors
# ----------------------------
Blood_datas_clean <- Blood_datas %>%
  mutate(
    Groupe = ifelse(Groupe == "Contrôle", "Control", Groupe),  # rename group for model
    week_factor = factor(week, levels = c("4", "6", "8")),
    Groupe_factor = factor(Groupe, levels = c("Winter", "Control", "Summer")),
    Sex_factor = factor(Sex),
    Shark_Id_factor = factor(Shark_Id)
  )

Blood_datas_clean <- Blood_datas_clean %>%
  mutate(
    Antibody_rank = rank(Antibody_titer, ties.method = "average")
  )


group_colors <- c("Winter" = "#FFF5E6", "Control" = "#FDB888", "Summer" = "#B30000")
Blood_datas_clean <- Blood_datas_clean %>%
  filter(Antibody_titer <= 10)
# ----------------------------
# 1) Fit LMM
# ----------------------------
lmm_Ab_rank <- lmer(
  Antibody_rank ~ Groupe_factor * week_factor + Sex_factor + (1 | Shark_Id_factor),
  data = Blood_datas_clean
)
# ----------------------------
# 2) Pairwise comparisons (CLD letters)
# ----------------------------

summary(lmm_Ab_rank)
print(lmm_Ab_rank)
anova(lmm_Ab_rank)
# ----------------------------
# 3) Compute boxplot stats for positioning letters
# ----------------------------
box_stats_rank <- Blood_datas_clean %>%
  group_by(week_factor, Groupe_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    ymin   = min(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(Antibody_titer, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
    middle = median(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(Antibody_titer, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
    ymax   = max(Antibody_titer, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# ----------------------------
# 4) Plot Antibody titer
# ----------------------------
p_Ab_rank <- ggplot(Blood_datas_clean,
                    aes(x = week_factor, y = Antibody_titer, fill = Groupe_factor)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, position = position_dodge(width = 0.75)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = Groupe_factor),
             shape = 21, size = 2, stroke = 0.5,
             position = position_jitterdodge(jitter.width = 0.1, dodge.width = 0.75),
             alpha = 0.8) +


  scale_fill_manual(values = group_colors,
                    labels = c("Winter" = "Winter",
                               "Control" = "Static",
                               "Summer" = "Summer")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Winter"="black",
                                "Control"="black",
                                "Summer"="black"),
                     guide = "none") +
  labs(x = "", y = "Antibody Titer", fill = "Group") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 15) +
  theme(
    axis.title = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.text.x = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
    axis.text.y = element_text(color = "black", size = 14),
     axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), # add axis lines
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"), # optional: ticks
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"), # add axis lines
    axis.ticks = element_line(color = "black"), # optional: ticks
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = "black", linewidth = 0.3),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 22)
  )+
  annotate(
    "text",                 # <--- specify type of annotation
    x = Inf, y = Inf,       # top-right corner
    label = "n.s.",          # <--- the text
    hjust = 1.1, vjust = 1.1,
    size = 10
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("Week 4", "Week 6", "Week 8")) +
  coord_cartesian(clip = "off")

print(p_Ab_rank)
# ----------------------------
# 5) Export
# ----------------------------
ggsave("Figure_antibody_final.png", p_Ab_rank, width = 15, height = 12, dpi = 300)
ggsave("Figure_antibody_final.pdf", p_Ab_rank, width = 15, height = 12)


```
